quality_rules: Templates, drafts, local-proof reports, and demo scenarios remain quarantined.
Use the same scenario id when you want BETA to judge progress against a baseline.
Record failed real runs too; failure history is useful project-management evidence.
Do not use AI advice as proof unless a real report, CI result, field note, or bench measurement supports it.
Firmware runs should include serial frame or operator evidence proving physical identity, board tag, MAC, temperature/battery health, and OLED state.

required_fields.why: Lets BETA accept the report as usable evidence instead of quarantining it.

required_fields.value: bench | field | ci | firmware | hardware | operator | measured
required_fields.why: Tells BETA what kind of real-world source produced the measurement.
Matching scenario ids allow before/after comparisons over time.
Correctness gates are the minimum proof before performance claims matter.
Sample size affects confidence in throughput, storage, and latency claims.
Keeps the metric tied to the actual test, bench note, CI run, or field observation.
Lets a reviewer trace the metric back to the file, capture, or note that produced it.

metric_definitions.label: Observation ingest throughput
metric_definitions.plain: How many observations the ingest path accepts per second.
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; compare against the previous matching scenario.
metric_definitions.why: Shows whether the system can keep up with incoming field data.

metric_definitions.label: Write p95
metric_definitions.plain: The slow end of write latency; 95 percent of writes were faster than this.
metric_definitions.target: Lower is better; under 100 ms currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_definitions.why: Captures ingest responsiveness without being fooled by only average latency.

metric_definitions.label: Query p95
metric_definitions.plain: The slow end of read/query latency; 95 percent of queries were faster than this.
metric_definitions.target: Lower is better; under 100 ms currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_definitions.why: Shows whether operators and dashboards will feel responsive.

metric_definitions.label: Station features
metric_definitions.plain: Count of station features represented in the proof output.
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; nonzero coverage earns effectiveness credit.
metric_definitions.why: Shows that station coverage is present rather than only raw observations.

metric_definitions.label: Bathymetry points
metric_definitions.plain: Count of bathymetry points available to the scenario.
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; should grow with richer scenarios.
metric_definitions.why: Shows that terrain/depth data is included in the proof evidence.

metric_definitions.label: Mesh routes
metric_definitions.plain: Count of mesh routes represented in the scenario.
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; nonzero routes earn effectiveness credit.
metric_definitions.why: Shows whether route/network behavior is being exercised.

metric_definitions.label: Portal payload
metric_definitions.plain: Size of the served portal shell.
metric_definitions.target: Lower is better; below 350 KB currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_definitions.why: Large payloads hurt slow-link and tunnel usability.

metric_definitions.label: DB bytes per observation
metric_definitions.plain: Database size divided by accepted observations.
metric_definitions.target: Lower is better; below 150 KB per observation currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_definitions.why: Shows storage efficiency and whether growth is becoming expensive.

metric_definitions.label: Wire savings
metric_definitions.plain: Estimated savings of the binary wire frame compared with JSON.
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; 60 percent or more currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_definitions.why: Shows whether the protocol is reducing transmission cost.

Choose the metric that would prove movement toward that goal.
Collect one real baseline report or source input.

Repeat the same scenario and compare the metric to the baseline.

project_goals: Use BETA to track and improve BETA itself
Measure whether BETA is becoming more useful, reliable, actionable, and reusable across projects
Identify missing project-management, QA, data-analysis, and AI-assistant capabilities
Keep BETA usable as a no-command project manager for OMEGA and BETA itself
Track real tests, evidence reports, todos, work sessions, and AI manager output as measurable project data
Improve BETA from concrete gaps found while using it on real projects
Prove OMEGA gateway proof runs pass required and advisory gates on real measured pipeline data
Track software, firmware, mesh, portal, and field-readiness progress from accepted evidence only
Use BETA to identify the next highest-value OMEGA test, source input, and blocker
metric_playbook.summary: BETA uses metrics for three jobs: prove the project works, prove it is improving, and decide what work deserves attention next.

metric_history.explanation: Observation ingest throughput needs a matching baseline before progress can be judged.
metric_history.label: Observation ingest throughput
metric_history.plain: How many observations the ingest path accepts per second.
metric_history.target: Higher is better; compare against the previous matching scenario.
metric_history.why: Shows whether the system can keep up with incoming field data.

metric_history.explanation: Write p95 is stable within the 2 percent noise band.
metric_history.label: Write p95
metric_history.plain: The slow end of write latency; 95 percent of writes were faster than this.
metric_history.target: Lower is better; under 100 ms currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_history.why: Captures ingest responsiveness without being fooled by only average latency.

metric_history.explanation: Query p95 is stable within the 2 percent noise band.
metric_history.label: Query p95
metric_history.plain: The slow end of read/query latency; 95 percent of queries were faster than this.
metric_history.target: Lower is better; under 100 ms currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_history.why: Shows whether operators and dashboards will feel responsive.

metric_history.explanation: Station features needs a matching baseline before progress can be judged.
metric_history.label: Station features
metric_history.plain: Count of station features represented in the proof output.
metric_history.target: Higher is better; nonzero coverage earns effectiveness credit.
metric_history.why: Shows that station coverage is present rather than only raw observations.

metric_history.explanation: Bathymetry points needs a matching baseline before progress can be judged.
metric_history.label: Bathymetry points
metric_history.plain: Count of bathymetry points available to the scenario.
metric_history.target: Higher is better; should grow with richer scenarios.
metric_history.why: Shows that terrain/depth data is included in the proof evidence.

metric_history.explanation: Mesh routes needs a matching baseline before progress can be judged.
metric_history.label: Mesh routes
metric_history.plain: Count of mesh routes represented in the scenario.
metric_history.target: Higher is better; nonzero routes earn effectiveness credit.
metric_history.why: Shows whether route/network behavior is being exercised.

metric_history.explanation: Portal payload is stable within the 2 percent noise band.
metric_history.label: Portal payload
metric_history.plain: Size of the served portal shell.
metric_history.target: Lower is better; below 350 KB currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_history.why: Large payloads hurt slow-link and tunnel usability.

metric_history.explanation: DB bytes per observation is stable within the 2 percent noise band.
metric_history.label: DB bytes per observation
metric_history.plain: Database size divided by accepted observations.
metric_history.target: Lower is better; below 150 KB per observation currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_history.why: Shows storage efficiency and whether growth is becoming expensive.

metric_history.explanation: Wire savings needs a matching baseline before progress can be judged.
metric_history.label: Wire savings
metric_history.plain: Estimated savings of the binary wire frame compared with JSON.
metric_history.target: Higher is better; 60 percent or more currently earns efficiency credit.
metric_history.why: Shows whether the protocol is reducing transmission cost.
